title: torilis nodosa subsp. bracteosa
intro: Torilis nodosa subsp. bracteosa, also known as Hemlock Water Dropwort, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve flatulence, and reduce inflammation. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as improving liver function, reducing fever, and acting as an antiseptic agent. Traditionally, Hemlock Water Dropwort has been prepared as an herbal tea or infused in oil, which is used topically to relieve skin irritations and promote wound healing.
